upstream_anchor_phrases.phrase: Seven words. That's the whole golf lesson.
Match the yellow ball to the white ball.
The equipment teaches. Your little legend plays.
Designed for coaching. Not for discovery.
My dad gave me this game.

fssit_candidates.statement: You don't have to teach them. At all.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Explains why backyard coaching failed — it wasn't the parent, it was the equipment assuming coaching was required
fssit_candidates.notes: Strongest FSSIT candidate. Directly violates every parent's assumption that teaching golf requires instruction.

fssit_candidates.statement: Every kids' club on the market was designed for coaching — but toddlers can't learn from coaching.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Names the gap no competitor has named — all equipment assumed verbal instruction
fssit_candidates.notes: Industry-level reframe. Makes the whole category wrong, not just cheap products.

fssit_candidates.statement: The same principle that teaches your kid a shape sorter can teach them golf.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Connects a learning experience every parent has witnessed to an unexpected application
fssit_candidates.notes: Bridge FSSIT. Creates 'of course!' recognition by connecting known success to new domain.

fssit_candidates.statement: Your kid didn't quit golf because it was hard — they quit because nobody designed equipment that speaks their language.
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Removes parent guilt and child blame simultaneously — the equipment was wrong, not the kid or the parent
fssit_candidates.notes: High NR but must be deployed carefully in hope market — cannot feel like blame or guilt.

fssit_candidates.statement: What if your only job was to play alongside them?
fssit_candidates.past_explaining_power: Reframes the parent's entire approach from instructor to companion
fssit_candidates.notes: Identity FSSIT. Taps the desire to BE a golf buddy rather than DO golf instruction.

tier1_expression_reference.voice_register: Grant talking to a fellow golf-loving parent at the range
tier1_expression_reference.pacing_signature: Social-native conversational flow, short sentences, natural transitions
